translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass after the death of Joshua that the children of Israel asked Jehovah, saying, Which of us shall go up against the Canaanites first, to fight against them?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said, Judah shall go up: behold, I have delivered the land into his hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Judah said to Simeon his brother, Come up with me into my lot, and let us fight against the Canaanites, and I likewise will go with thee into thy lot; and Simeon went with him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Judah went up; and Jehovah delivered the Canaanites and the Perizzites into their hand, and they smote them in Bezek, ten thousand men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they found Adoni-Bezek in Bezek, and fought against him, and they smote the Canaanites and the Perizzites.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Adoni-Bezek fled, and they pursued after him, and caught him, and cut off his thumbs and his great toes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Adoni-Bezek said, Seventy kings, with their thumbs and their great toes cut off, gleaned under my table: as I have done, soGod has requited me. And they brought him to Jerusalem, and there he died.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Judah fought against Jerusalem, and took it, and smote it with the edge of the sword, and set the city on fire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And afterwards the children of Judah went down to fight against the Canaanites, that dwelt in the hill-country, and in the south, and in the lowland.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Judah went against the Canaanites that dwelt in Hebron — the name of Hebron before was Kirjath-Arba; and they slew Sheshai and Ahiman and Talmai.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And from there he went against the inhabitants of Debir; now the name of Debir before was Kirjath-sepher.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Caleb said, He that smites Kirjath-sepher and takes it, to him will I give Achsah my daughter as wife.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Othniel the son of Kenaz, Caleb's younger brother, took it; and he gave him Achsah his daughter as wife.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass as she came, that she urged him to ask of her father the field; and she sprang down from the ass. And Caleb said to her, What wouldest thou?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she said to him, Give me a blessing; for thou hast given me a southern land; give me also springs of water. And Caleb gave her the upper springs and the lower springs.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of the Kenite, Moses' father-in-law, had gone up out of the city of palm-trees with the children of Judah into the wilderness of Judah, which is in the south of Arad; and they went and dwelt with the people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Judah went with Simeon his brother, and they slew the Canaanites that inhabited Zephath, and utterly destroyed it; and they called the name of the city Hormah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Judah took Gazah and its border, and Ashkelon and its border, and Ekron and its border.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah was with Judah; and he took possession of the hill-country, for he did not dispossess the inhabitants of the valley, because they had chariots of iron.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they gave to Caleb Hebron, as Moses had said; and he dispossessed from thence the three sons of Anak.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Benjamin did not dispossess the Jebusites, the inhabitants of Jerusalem; but the Jebusites dwell with the children of Benjamin in Jerusalem to this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the house of Joseph, they also went up against Bethel; and Jehovah was with them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the house of Joseph sent to search out Bethel; now the name of the city before was Luz.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the guards saw a man come forth out of the city, and said unto him, Shew us, we pray thee, how [we] may enter into the city, and we will shew thee kindness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he shewed them how to enter into the city. And they smote the city with the edge of the sword; but they let go the man and all his family.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the man went into the land of the Hittites, and built a city, and called its name Luz, which is its name to this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Manasseh did not dispossess Beth-shean and its dependent villages, nor Taanach and its dependent villages, nor the inhabitants of Dor and its dependent villages, nor the inhabitants of Ibleam and its dependent villages, nor the inhabitants of Megiddo and its dependent villages; and the Canaanites would dwell in that land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when Israel became strong, that they made the Canaanites tributary; but they did not utterly dispossess them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Ephraim did not dispossess the Canaanites that dwelt in Gezer; but the Canaanites dwelt among them in Gezer.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Zebulun did not dispossess the inhabitants of Kitron, nor the inhabitants of Nahalol; but the Canaanites dwelt among them, and became tributaries.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Asher did not dispossess the inhabitants of Accho, nor the inhabitants of Zidon, nor Ahlab, nor Achzib, nor Helbah, nor Aphik, nor Rehob;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the Asherites dwelt among the Canaanites, the inhabitants of the land; for they did not dispossess them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Naphtali did not dispossess the inhabitants of Beth-shemesh, nor the inhabitants of Beth-anath; and he dwelt among the Canaanites, the inhabitants of the land, but the inhabitants of Beth-shemesh and of Beth-anath became tributaries to them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Amorites forced the children of Dan into the hill-country, for they would not suffer them to come down to the valley.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Amorites would dwell on mount Heres, in Ajalon and in Shaalbim; but the hand of the house of Joseph prevailed, and they became tributaries.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the border of the Amorites was from the ascent of Akrabbim, from the rock, and upwards.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Angel of Jehovah came up from Gilgal to Bochim, and said, I made you to go up out of Egypt, and have brought you to the land which I swore unto your fathers; and I said, I will never break my covenant with you; and as for you,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: ye shall make no covenant with the inhabitants of this land; ye shall throw down their altars: but ye have not hearkened unto my voice. Why have ye done this?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wherefore I also said, I will not drive them out from before you; but they shall be [scourges] in your sides, and theirgods shall be a snare unto you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass, when the Angel of Jehovah spoke these words to all the children of Israel, that the people lifted up their voice and wept.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they called the name of that place Bochim; and they sacrificed there to Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua dismissed the people, and the children of Israel went every man to his inheritance to possess the land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the people served Jehovah all the days of Joshua, and all the days of the elders whose days were prolonged after Joshua, who had seen all the great works of Jehovah, which he had done for Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joshua the son of Nun, the servant of Jehovah, died, a hundred and ten years old.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they buried him in the border of his inheritance in Timnath-Heres, in mount Ephraim, on the north side of the mountain of Gaash.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And also all that generation were gathered to their fathers; and there arose another generation after them, which knew not Jehovah, nor yet the works which he had done for Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel did evil in the sight of Jehovah, and served the Baals.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they forsook Jehovah theGod of their fathers, who had brought them up out of the land of Egypt, and followed othergods of thegods of the peoples that were round about them, and bowed themselves to them, and provoked Jehovah to anger.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they forsook Jehovah, and served Baal and the Ashtoreths.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the anger of Jehovah was hot against Israel, and he delivered them into the hands of spoilers that spoiled them, and he sold them into the hands of their enemies round about; and they could not any longer stand before their enemies.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whithersoever they went out the hand of Jehovah was against them for evil, as Jehovah had said, and as Jehovah had sworn unto them; and they were greatly distressed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah raised up judges, and they saved them out of the hand of those that spoiled them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But they did not even hearken to their judges, for they went a whoring after othergods, and bowed themselves to them; they turned quickly out of the way that their fathers had walked in, obeying the commandments of Jehovah; they did not so.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when Jehovah raised them up judges, then Jehovah was with the judge, and saved them out of the hand of their enemies all the days of the judge; for it repented Jehovah because of their groanings by reason of them that oppressed them and crushed them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when the judge died, that they turned back and corrupted themselves more than their fathers, in following othergods to serve them, and to bow down to them: they ceased not from their own doings, nor from their stubborn way.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the anger of Jehovah was hot against Israel; and he said, Because this nation hath transgressed my covenant which I commanded their fathers, and hath not hearkened unto my voice,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I also will not henceforth dispossess from before them any of the nations that Joshua left when he died;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that through them I may prove Israel, whether they will keep the way of Jehovah to walk therein, as their fathers did keep [it], or not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore Jehovah left those nations, without dispossessing them hastily, neither delivered he them into the hand of Joshua.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And these are the nations that Jehovah left, to prove Israel by them, all that had not known all the wars of Canaan;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: only that the generations of the children of Israel might know war by learning it, at the least those who before had known nothing thereof:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: five lord ships of the Philistines, and all the Canaanites, and the Sidonians, and the Hivites that dwell on mount Lebanon; from mount Baal-Hermon unto the entering into Hamath.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they were to prove Israel by them, to know whether they would obey the commandments of Jehovah, which he commanded their fathers by the hand of Moses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel dwelt among the Canaanites: Hittites and Amorites and Perizzites and Hivites and Jebusites;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they took their daughters as wives, and gave their daughters to their sons, and served theirgods.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel did evil in the sight of Jehovah, and forgot Jehovah theirGod, and served the Baals and the Asherahs.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the anger of Jehovah was hot against Israel, and he sold them into the hand of Chushan-rishathaim king of Mesopotamia; and the children of Israel served Chushan-rishathaim eight years.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel cried to Jehovah; and Jehovah raised up a saviour to the children of Israel, who saved them, Othniel the son of Kenaz, Caleb's younger brother.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Spirit of Jehovah was upon him, and he judged Israel; and he went out to war, and Jehovah gave Chushan-rishathaim king of Syria into his hand; and his hand prevailed against Chushan-rishathaim.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the land had rest forty years; and Othniel the son of Kenaz died.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel again did evil in the sight of Jehovah; and Jehovah strengthened Eglon the king of Moab against Israel, because they did evil in the sight of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he gathered to him the children of Ammon and Amalek and went and smote Israel, and they took possession of the city of palm-trees.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel served Eglon the king of Moab eighteen years.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel cried to Jehovah, and Jehovah raised them up a saviour, Ehud the son of Gera, the Benjaminite, a man left-handed. And by him the children of Israel sent a gift to Eglon king of Moab.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Ehud made him a sword having two edges, it was of a cubit length; and he girded it under his raiment upon his right hip.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he brought the gift to Eglon king of Moab; now Eglon was a very fat man.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when he had ended offering the gift, he sent away the people that had borne the gift.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he turned from the graven images that were by Gilgal, and said, I have a secret word unto thee, O king. And he said, Be silent! And all that stood by him went out from him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Ehud came to him; now he was sitting in the cool upper-chamber, which was for him alone. And Ehud said, I have a word fromGod unto thee. And he arose from the seat.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Ehud put forth his left hand, and took the sword from his right hip, and thrust it into his belly;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the haft also went in after the blade, and the fat closed upon the blade; for he did not draw the sword out of his belly, and it came out between the legs.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Ehud went out into the portico, and shut the doors of the upper-chamber upon him, and bolted them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when he was gone out, the servants of the [king] came and saw, and behold, the doors of the upper-chamber were bolted. And they said, Surely he is covering his feet in the summer chamber.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they waited till they were ashamed; and behold, he opened not the doors of the upper-chamber, and they took the key, and opened [them], and behold, their lord lay dead on the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Ehud had escaped while they lingered, and passed beyond the graven images, and escaped to Seirah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when he was come, that he blew a trumpet in the hill-country of Ephraim, and the children of Israel went down with him from the hill-country, and he before them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, Follow after me, for Jehovah has delivered your enemies the Moabites into your hand. And they went down after him, and took the fords of the Jordan toward Moab, and suffered no one to pass over.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they slew the Moabites at that time, about ten thousand men, all fat, and all men of valour, and not a man escaped.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moab was subdued that day under the hand of Israel. And the land had rest eighty years.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And after him was Shamgar the son of Anath; and he smote the Philistines, six hundred men, with an ox-goad. And he also delivered Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel again did evil in the sight of Jehovah; now Ehud was dead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah sold them into the hand of Jabin king of Canaan, who reigned in Hazor; and the captain of his army was Sisera, who dwelt in Harosheth-Goim.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel cried to Jehovah; for he had nine hundred chariots of iron, and he mightily oppressed the children of Israel twenty years.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Deborah, a prophetess, the wife of Lapidoth, judged Israel at that time.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she dwelt under the palm-tree of Deborah between Ramah and Bethel in mount Ephraim; and the children of Israel came up to her for judgment.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she sent and called Barak the son of Abinoam out of Kedesh-Naphtali, and said to him, Hath not Jehovah theGod of Israel commanded? Go and draw towards mount Tabor, and take with thee ten thousand men of the children of Naphtali and of the children of Zebulun,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and I will draw unto thee, to the torrent Kishon, Sisera, the captain of Jabin's army, and his chariots and his multitude, and I will give him into thy hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Barak said to her, If thou goest with me, then I will go, but if thou goest not with me, I will not go.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she said, I will by all means go with thee, only that it will not be to thine honour upon the way which thou goest, for Jehovah will sell Sisera into the hand of a woman. And Deborah arose, and went with Barak to Kedesh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Barak called together Zebulun and Naphtali to Kedesh; and there went up at his feet ten thousand men; and Deborah went up with him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (Now Heber the Kenite had severed himself from the Kenites, from the children of Hobab the father-in-law of Moses, and had pitched his tent as far as the oak of Zaannaim, which is by Kedesh.)

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they told Sisera that Barak the son of Abinoam had gone up to mount Tabor.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Sisera gathered together all his chariots, nine hundred chariots of iron, and all the people that were with him, from Harosheth-Goim to the torrent Kishon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Deborah said to Barak, Up; for this is the day in which Jehovah hath given Sisera into thy hand! Is not Jehovah gone out before thee? And Barak went down from mount Tabor, and ten thousand men after him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah discomfited Sisera, and all the chariots, and all the army, with the edge of the sword before Barak; and Sisera got down from [his] chariot, and fled on foot.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Barak pursued after the chariots, and after the army, to Harosheth-Goim; and all the army of Sisera fell by the edge of the sword; not one was left.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Sisera fled on foot to the tent of Jael the wife of Heber the Kenite; for there was peace between Jabin the king of Hazor and the house of Heber the Kenite.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jael went out to meet Sisera, and said to him, Turn in, my lord, turn in to me; fear not. And he turned in to her, into the tent, and she covered him with the quilt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to her, Give me, I pray thee, a little water to drink, for I am thirsty. And she opened the flask of milk, and gave him drink, and covered him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to her, Stand in the door of the tent, and it shall be if any one come and inquire of thee, and say, Is there any man here? that thou shalt say, No.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jael Heber's wife took a tent-pin, and took the hammer in her hand, and went softly to him, and smote the pin into his temples, and it penetrated into the ground; for he had fallen into a deep sleep and was faint; and he died.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And behold, as Barak pursued Sisera, Jael went out to meet him, and said to him, Come, and I will shew thee the man whom thou seekest. And he went into her [tent], and behold, Sisera lay dead, and the pin was in his temples.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: SoGod subdued on that day Jabin king of Canaan before the children of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the hand of the children of Israel ever advanced, and prevailed against Jabin king of Canaan, until they had cut off Jabin king of Canaan.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then sang Deborah and Barak, the son of Abinoam, on that day, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For that leaders led in Israel, For that the people willingly offered themselves, Bless Jehovah!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hear, ye kings; give ear, ye princes, I, [even] I, will sing to Jehovah; I will hymn to Jehovah theGod of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah, when thou wentest forth from Seir, When thou marchedst out of the fields of Edom, The earth trembled, and the heavens dropped, Yea, the clouds dropped water.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The mountains quaked before the face of Jehovah, That Sinai, from before Jehovah theGod of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In the days of Shamgar the son of Anath, in the days of Jael, The roads were unused, and the travellers on highways went by crooked paths.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The villages ceased in Israel, Ceased until that I Deborah arose, That I arose a mother in Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They chose newgods; then was war in the gates: Was there a shield or spear seen among forty thousand in Israel?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My heart is toward the governors of Israel, who offered themselves willingly among the people. Bless Jehovah!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye that ride on white she-asses, ye that sit on carpets, and ye that walk by the way, consider.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Because of the voice of those who divide [the spoil] in the midst of the places of drawing water; There they rehearse the righteous acts of Jehovah, His righteous acts toward his villages in Israel. Then the people of Jehovah went down to the gates.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Awake, awake, Deborah! awake, awake, utter a song! Arise, Barak, and lead captive thy captives, thou son of Abinoam!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then come down, thou, the remnant of nobles, [as his] people; Jehovah! come down with me in the midst of the mighty ones.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Out of Ephraim [came] those whose root was in Amalek; After thee was Benjamin among thy peoples. Out of Machir came down governors, And out of Zebulun they that handled the staff of the ruler.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the princes in Issachar were with Deborah; And Issachar, like Barak; They were sent into the valley at his feet. In the divisions of Reuben there were great resolves of heart!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Why abodest thou among the sheepfolds, To hear the bleating of the flocks? In the divisions of Reuben there were great deliberations of heart!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Gilead abode beyond Jordan; And Dan, why did he remain in ships? Asher sat on the sea-shore, And abode in his creeks.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Zebulun is a people [that] jeoparded their lives unto death, Naphtali also, on the high places of the field.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Kings came, — they fought; Then fought the kings of Canaan; At Taanach, by the waters of Megiddo; They took no spoil of silver.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: From heaven was the fight; The stars from their courses fought with Sisera.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The torrent of Kishon swept them away, That ancient torrent, the torrent Kishon. My soul, thou hast trodden down strength!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then did the horse-hoofs clatter with the coursings, The coursings of their steeds.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Curse Meroz, saith the Angel of Jehovah; Curse, curse the inhabitants thereof; For they came not to the help of Jehovah, To the help of Jehovah among the mighty.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Blessed above women shall Jael the wife of Heber the Kenite be, Blessed above women in the tent!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He asked water, she gave milk; In the nobles' bowl she brought forth cream.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: She put her hand to the tent-pin, And her right hand to the workmen's hammer; And she smote Sisera, she struck through his head, Shattered and pierced through his temples.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Between her feet he bowed, he fell, he lay down: Between her feet he bowed, he fell; Where he bowed, there he fell, overcome.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Them other of Sisera looketh out at the window, And crieth through the lattice, Why is his chariot so long in coming? Why tarry the trampings of his chariots?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The wise amongst her ladies answer [her], Yea, she returneth answer to herself,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Have they not found, divided the booty, A damsel, two damsels, to each? A booty of dyed stuffs for Sisera, A booty of dyed stuffs of embroidery, Dyed stuff of double embroidery for the neck of a spoiler?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So let all thine enemies perish, Jehovah! But let them that love him be as the rising of the sun in its might. And the land had rest forty years.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel did evil in the sight of Jehovah; and Jehovah delivered them into the hand of Midian seven years.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the hand of Midian prevailed against Israel. Because of the Midianites the children of Israel made for themselves the dens that are in the mountains, and the caves, and the strongholds.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when Israel sowed, that Midian came up, and Amalek, and the children of the east, and came up against them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they encamped against them, and destroyed the produce of the land, until thou come to Gazah, and they left no sustenance in Israel, neither sheep, nor ox, nor ass.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For they came up with their cattle and their tents, and they came as locusts for multitude; both they and their camels were without number; and they entered into the land to destroy it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Israel was greatly impoverished because of Midian. And the children of Israel cried to Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when the children of Israel cried to Jehovah because of Midian,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that Jehovah sent a prophet to the children of Israel, who said to them, Thus saith Jehovah theGod of Israel: I brought you up from Egypt, and brought you forth out of the house of bondage;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and I delivered you out of the hand of the Egyptians, and out of the hand of all that oppressed you, and drove them out from before you, and gave you their land,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and I said to you, I am Jehovah yourGod; fear not thegods of the Amorites, in whose land ye dwell. But ye have not hearkened to my voice.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And an angel of Jehovah came and sat under the terebinth that was in Ophrah, that [belonged] to Joash the Abi-ezrite. And his son Gideon threshed wheat in the winepress, to secure [it] from the Midianites.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Angel of Jehovah appeared to him, and said to him, Jehovah is with thee, thou mighty man of valour.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Gideon said to him, Ah my Lord, if Jehovah be with us, why then is all this befallen us? and where are all his miracles that our fathers told us of, saying, Did not Jehovah bring us up from Egypt? And now Jehovah hath cast us off, and given us into the hand of Midian.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah looked upon him, and said, Go in this thy might, and thou shalt save Israel from the hand of Midian. Have not I sent thee?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to him, Ah Lord, wherewith shall I save Israel? behold, my thousand is the poorest in Manasseh, and I am the least in my father's house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to him, I will certainly be with thee; and thou shalt smite Midian as one man.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to him, If now I have found favour in thine eyes, shew me a sign that it is thou who talkest with me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Depart not hence, I pray thee, until I come unto thee, and bring forth my present, and set it before thee. And he said, I will tarry until thou come again.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Gideon went in, and made ready a kid of the goats, and an ephah of flour in unleavened cakes: the flesh he put in a basket, and he put the broth in a pot, and brought it out to him under the terebinth, and presented it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Angel ofGod said to him, Take the flesh and the unleavened cakes, and lay them upon this rock, and pour out the broth. And he did so.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Angel of Jehovah put forth the end of the staff that was in his hand, and touched the flesh and the unleavened cakes; and there rose up fire out of the rock, and consumed the flesh and the unleavened cakes. And the Angel of Jehovah departed out of his sight.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Gideon perceived that he was an angel of Jehovah; and Gideon said, Alas, Lord Jehovah! for because I have seen an angel of Jehovah face to face...

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to him, Peace be unto thee: fear not; thou shalt not die.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Gideon built there an altar to Jehovah, and called it Jehovah-shalom. To this day it is yet in Ophrah of the Abi-ezrites.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass the same night, that Jehovah said to him, Take the young bullock, which thy father hath, even the second bullock of seven years old, and throw down the altar of Baal that thy father hath, and cut down the Asherah that is by it;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and build an altar to Jehovah thyGod upon the top of this strong place in the ordered manner, and take the second bullock, and offer up a burnt-offering with the wood of the Asherah that thou shalt cut down.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Gideon took ten men of his servants, and did as Jehovah had said to him. And it came to pass, because he feared his father's house, and the men of the city, if he did it by day, that he did it by night.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when the men of the city arose early in the morning, behold, the altar of Baal was broken down, and the Asherah was cut down that was by it, and the second bullock was offered up upon the altar that was built.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said one to another, Who has done this thing? And when they inquired and asked, they said, Gideon the son of Joash has done this thing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the men of the city said to Joash, Bring out thy son, that he may die, because he has broken down the altar of Baal, and because he has cut down the Asherah that was by it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joash said to all that stood near him, Will ye contend for Baal? or will ye save him? he that contends for him, let him be put to death whilst it is yet morning. If he be agod, let him plead for himself, because they have broken down his altar.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And on that day they called him Jerubbaal, saying, Let Baal plead with him, because he has broken down his altar.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all Midian and Amalek and the children of the east were gathered together, and went over, and encamped in the valley of Jezreel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Spirit of Jehovah came upon Gideon, and he blew the trumpet, and the Abi-ezrites were gathered after him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he sent messengers throughout Manasseh, and they also were gathered after him; and he sent messengers to Asher, and to Zebulun, and to Naphtali; and they came up to meet them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Gideon said toGod, If thou wilt save Israel by my hand, as thou hast said,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: behold, I put a fleece of wool on the threshing-floor; if dew shall be on the fleece only, and it be dry upon all the ground, then shall I know that thou wilt save Israel by my hand, as thou hast said.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it was so. And when he rose up early on the morrow, he pressed the fleece together, and wrung dew out of the fleece, a bowl-full of water.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Gideon said toGod, Let not thine anger be hot against me, and I will speak but this once! Let me prove, I pray thee, but this once with the fleece; let it, I pray thee, be dry upon the fleece only, and upon all the ground let there be dew.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: AndGod did so that night, and it was dry upon the fleece only, but on all the ground there was dew.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jerubbaal, who is Gideon, arose early, and all the people that were with him, and they encamped beside the spring Harod; and he had the camp of Midian on the north by the hill of Moreh in the valley.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Gideon, The people that are with thee are too many for me to give Midian into their hand, lest Israel vaunt themselves against me, saying, Mine own hand hath saved me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now proclaim in the ears of the people, saying, Whoever is timid and afraid, let him go back and turn from mount Gilead. And there went back of the people twenty-two thousand; and there remained ten thousand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Gideon, Still the people are many; bring them down to the water, and I will try them for thee there, and it shall be, that of whom I shall say unto thee, This shall go with thee, the same shall go with thee; and of whomsoever I shall say unto thee, This shall not go with thee, the same shall not go.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he brought down the people to the water; and Jehovah said to Gideon, Every one that lappeth of the water with his tongue, as a dog lappeth, him shalt thou set by himself; likewise every one that boweth down on his knees to drink.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the number of them that lapped, with their hand to their mouth, were three hundred men; and all the rest of the people bowed down on their knees to drink water.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Gideon, By the three hundred men that lapped will I save you, and give Midian into thy hand; and let all the people go every man to his place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they took the victuals of the people in their hand, and their trumpets; and all the men of Israel he sent away, every man to his tent, but retained the three hundred men. Now the camp of Midian was beneath him in the valley.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass in that night, that Jehovah said to him, Arise, go down to the camp; for I have given it into thy hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if thou fear to go down, go thou with Phurah thy servant down to the camp;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and thou shalt hear what they say; and afterwards shall thy hand be strengthened, and thou shalt go down unto the camp. And he went down with Phurah his servant to the outside of the armed men that were in the camp.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Midian and Amalek and all the children of the east lay along in the valley as locusts for multitude; and their camels were without number, as the sand upon the sea-shore for multitude.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Gideon came, and behold, a man was telling a dream to his fellow; and he said, Behold, I dreamed a dream, and lo, a cake of barley-bread tumbled into the camp of Midian, and came to the tent, and smote it that it fell, and overturned it; and the tent lay along.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his fellow answered and said, This is nothing else save the sword of Gideon the son of Joash, the man of Israel:God hath given into his hand Midian and all the host.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when Gideon heard the telling of the dream and its interpretation, that he worshipped. And he returned into the camp of Israel, and said, Arise; for Jehovah hath given into your hand the camp of Midian.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he divided the three hundred men into three companies, and he put a trumpet in every man's hand, and empty pitchers, and torches within the pitchers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, Look on me, and do likewise; behold, when I come to the extremity of the camp, it shall be that, as I do, so shall ye do.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when I blow with a trumpet, I and all that are with me, ye also shall blow the trumpets around the whole camp, and shall say, For Jehovah and for Gideon!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Gideon, and the hundred men that were with him, came to the outside of the camp in the beginning of the middle watch; and they had but newly set the watch; and they blew the trumpets, and broke the pitchers that were in their hands.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the three companies blew the trumpets, and broke in pieces the pitchers, and held the torches in their left hand, and the trumpets in their right hand for blowing, and cried, The sword of Jehovah and of Gideon!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they stood every man in his place round about the camp; and all the host ran, and cried out, and fled.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the three hundred blew the trumpets, and Jehovah set every man's sword against his fellow, even throughout the camp. And the host fled to Beth-shittah towards Zererah, to the border of Abel-meholah, by Tabbath.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the men of Israel were called together out of Naphtali, and out of Asher, and out of all Manasseh, and pursued after Midian.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Gideon sent messengers throughout mount Ephraim, saying, Come down against Midian, and take before them the waters unto Beth-barah, and the Jordan. And all the men of Ephraim were called together, and took the waters unto Beth-barah, and the Jordan.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they took two princes of Midian, Oreb and Zeeb; and they slew Oreb upon the rock Oreb, and Zeeb they slew at the winepress of Zeeb; and they pursued Midian, and brought the heads of Oreb and Zeeb to Gideon beyond the Jordan.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the men of Ephraim said to him, What is this thing thou hast done to us, that thou calledst us not, when thou wentest to fight with Midian? And they disputed with him sharply.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, What have I done now in comparison with you? Are not the gleanings of Ephraim better than the vintage of Abi-ezer?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Into your hands hathGod delivered the princes of Midian, Oreb and Zeeb; and what was I able to do in comparison with you? Then their spirit was appeased toward him, when he said that word.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Gideon came to the Jordan, [and] passed over, he and the three hundred men that were with him, faint, yet pursuing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to the men of Succoth, Give, I pray you, loaves of bread to the people that follow me, for they are faint; and I am pursuing after Zebah and Zalmunna, kings of Midian.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the chief men of Succoth said, Are the hands of Zebah and Zalmunna already in thy hand, that we should give bread to thine army?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Gideon said, Therefore when Jehovah delivers Zebah and Zalmunna into my hand, I will thresh your flesh with thorns of the wilderness and with briars.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he went up thence to Penuel, and spoke to them in like manner. And the men of Penuel answered him as the men of Succoth had answered.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he spoke also to the men of Penuel, saying, When I come again in peace, I will break down this tower.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now Zebah and Zalmunna were in Karkor, and their camp with them, about fifteen thousand [men], all that were left of the whole camp of the children of the east; for there had fallen a hundred and twenty thousand men that drew sword.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Gideon went up by the way of them that dwell in tents on the east of Nobah and Jogbehah, and smote the camp; for the camp was at its ease.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Zebah and Zalmunna fled, and he pursued after them, and he took the two kings of Midian, Zebah and Zalmunna, and discomfited all the camp.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Gideon the son of Joash returned from the battle, from the ascent of Heres.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he caught a youth of the men of Succoth, and inquired of him; and he wrote down for him the chief men of Succoth, and the elders thereof, seventy-seven men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he came to the men of Succoth, and said, Behold Zebah and Zalmunna, with whom ye did upbraid me, saying, Are the hands of Zebah and Zalmunna already in thy hand, that we should give bread to thy men that are weary?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he took the elders of the city, and thorns of the wilderness and briars, and with them he taught the men of Succoth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he broke down the tower of Penuel, and slew the men of the city.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then said he to Zebah and Zalmunna, What sort of men were they that ye slew at Tabor? And they answered, As thou art, so were they; each one resembled the sons of a king.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, They were my brethren, the sons of my mother. [As] Jehovah liveth, if ye had saved them alive, I would not slay you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to Jether his firstborn, Arise, slay them! But the youth drew not his sword; for he feared, because he was yet a youth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Zebah and Zalmunna said, Rise thou, and fall on us; for as is the man, so is his strength. And Gideon arose, and slew Zebah and Zalmunna; and he took the moons that were on their camels' necks.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the men of Israel said to Gideon, Rule over us, both thou, and thy son, and thy son's son also; for thou hast saved us from the hand of Midian.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Gideon said to them, I will not rule over you, neither shall my son rule over you: Jehovah will rule over you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Gideon said to them, I would desire a request of you: give me every man the earrings of his booty; for they had golden earrings, because they were Ishmaelites.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said, We will willingly give [them]. And they spread a garment, and cast therein every man the earrings of his booty.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the weight of the golden earrings that he requested was a thousand seven hundred [shekels] of gold; besides the moons, and eardrops, and the purple garments that were on the kings of Midian, and besides the chains that were about their camels' necks.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Gideon made an ephod of them, and put it in his city, in Ophrah. And all Israel went thither a whoring after it; and it became a snare to Gideon, and to his house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Midian was subdued before the children of Israel, and they lifted up their heads no more. And the land had rest forty years in the days of Gideon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jerubbaal the son of Joash went and dwelt in his house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now Gideon had seventy sons who had come out of his loins, for he had many wives.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his concubine that was in Shechem, she also bore him a son, and he gave him the name of Abimelech.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Gideon the son of Joash died in a good old age, and was buried in the sepulchre of Joash his father, in Ophrah of the Abi-ezrites.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when Gideon was dead, that the children of Israel turned again, and went a whoring after the Baals, and set up Baal-Berith as theirgod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel remembered not Jehovah theirGod, who had delivered them out of the hand of all their enemies on every side.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they shewed no kindness to the house of Jerubbaal-Gideon, according to all the good that he had done to Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abimelech son of Jerubbaal went to Shechem to his mother's brethren, and spoke to them, and to all the family of the house of his mother's father, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Speak, I pray you, in the ears of all the citizens of Shechem, Which is better for you, that seventy persons, all sons of Jerubbaal, rule over you, or that one man rule over you? And remember that I am your bone and your flesh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his mother's brethren spoke of him in the ears of all the citizens of Shechem all these words. And their heart inclined after Abimelech; for they said, He is our brother.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they gave him seventy pieces of silver out of the house of Baal-Berith, and Abimelech hired with them vain and wanton men, and they followed him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he came to his father's house, to Ophrah, and slew his brethren the sons of Jerubbaal, seventy persons upon one stone; but there remained Jotham the youngest son of Jerubbaal; for he had hid himself.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the citizens of Shechem gathered together, and all the house of Millo, and went and made Abimelech king, by the memorial-oak that is in Shechem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they told it to Jotham, and he went and stood on the top of mount Gerizim, and lifted up his voice, and cried, and said to them, Hearken to me, ye citizens of Shechem, thatGod may hearken to you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The trees once went forth to anoint a king over them; and they said to the olive-tree, Reign over us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the olive-tree said to them, Should I leave my fatness, wherewith by me they honourGod and man, and go to wave over the trees?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the trees said to the fig-tree, Come thou, reign over us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the fig-tree said to them, Should I leave my sweetness, and my good fruit, and go to wave over the trees?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then said the trees to the vine, Come thou, reign over us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the vine said to them, Should I leave my new wine, which cheersGod and man, and go to wave over the trees?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then said all the trees to the thorn-bush, Come thou, reign over us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the thorn-bush said to the trees, If in truth ye anoint me king over you, come, put confidence in my shadow; but if not, fire shall come out of the thorn-bush and devour the cedars of Lebanon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now therefore, if ye have dealt truly and sincerely in that ye have made Abimelech king, and if ye have dealt well with Jerubbaal and his house, and if ye have done to him according to the deserving of his hands;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — for my father fought for you, and endangered his life, and delivered you out of the hand of Midian;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but ye are risen up against my father's house this day, and have slain his sons, seventy men, upon one stone, and have made Abimelech, the son of his handmaid, king over the citizens of Shechem, because he is your brother;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — if ye then have dealt truly and sincerely with Jerubbaal and with his house this day, rejoice ye in Abimelech, and let him also rejoice in you;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but if not, let fire come out from Abimelech, and devour the citizens of Shechem and the house of Millo; and let fire come out from the citizens of Shechem and from the house of Millo, and devour Abimelech.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jotham ran away, and fled, and went to Beer, and dwelt there, because of Abimelech his brother.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abimelech ruled over Israel three years.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: AndGod sent an evil spirit between Abimelech and the citizens of Shechem; and the citizens of Shechem dealt treacherously with Abimelech,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that the violence [done] to the seventy sons of Jerubbaal might come, and their blood be laid upon Abimelech their brother, who slew them, and upon the citizens of Shechem, who had strengthened his hands to slay his brethren.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the citizens of Shechem set liers in wait for him on the tops of the mountains, and they robbed all that came along that way by them. And it was told Abimelech.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Gaal the son of Ebed came with his brethren, and went over to Shechem; and the citizens of Shechem put confidence in him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they went out into the fields, and gathered their vineyards, and trode [the grapes], and made rejoicings, and went into the house of theirgod, and ate and drank, and cursed Abimelech.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Gaal the son of Ebed said, Who is Abimelech, and who is Shechem, that we should serve him? is he not the son of Jerubbaal? and Zebul his overseer? Serve the men of Hamor the father of Shechem! and why should we serve him?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Oh! would that this people were under my hand! then would I remove Abimelech. And he said to Abimelech, Increase thine army, and come out.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Zebul the ruler of the city heard the words of Gaal the son of Ebed, and his anger was kindled;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he sent messengers to Abimelech craftily, saying, Behold, Gaal the son of Ebed and his brethren are come to Shechem, and behold, they shut up the city against thee;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and now, rise up by night, thou and the people that are with thee, and lie in ambush in the fields.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall be in the morning when the sun is up, thou shalt rise early, and fall upon the city; and behold, he and the people that is with him shall come out against thee, and thou shalt do with him as thou shalt find occasion.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abimelech rose up, and all the people that were with him, by night, and they lay in ambush against Shechem in four companies.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Gaal the son of Ebed went out, and stood in the entrance of the gate of the city. Then Abimelech rose up, and the people that were with him, out of the ambush.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Gaal saw the people, and said to Zebul, Behold, people are coming down from the tops of the mountains. And Zebul said to him, Thou seest the shadow of the mountains as men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Gaal spoke again, and said, Behold, people are coming down from the high part of the land, and one company is coming along by the way of the Magician's oak.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then said Zebul to him, Where is now thy mouth, thou that saidst, Who is Abimelech, that we should serve him? is not this the people that thou hast despised? go out now, I pray, and fight against them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Gaal went out before the citizens of Shechem, and fought against Abimelech.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abimelech pursued him, and he fled before him, and there fell many wounded, as far as the entrance of the gate.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abimelech dwelt at Arumah. And Zebul drove out Gaal and his brethren, that they might not dwell in Shechem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass on the morrow, that the people went out into the field; and they told Abimelech.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he took the people, and divided them into three companies, and lay in ambush in the field. And he looked, and behold, the people came forth out of the city; and he rose up against them and smote them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abimelech, and the companies that were with him, rushed forward, and stood in the entrance of the gate of the city; and two of the companies ran upon all that were in the fields, and slew them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abimelech fought against the city all that day; and he took the city, and slew the people that were in it, and broke down the city, and sowed it with salt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the men of the tower of Shechem heard [that], and they entered into the stronghold of the house of thegod Berith.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it was told Abimelech that all the men of the tower of Shechem had gathered together.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Abimelech went up to mount Zalmon, he and all the people that were with him; and Abimelech took an axe in his hand, and cut down a bough from the trees, and took it up and laid it on his shoulder, and said to the people that was with him, What ye have seen me do, make haste, do as I have done.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the people likewise cut down every man his bough, and they followed Abimelech, and put [them] to the hold, and burned the hold with fire upon them. And all the men of the tower of Shechem died also, about a thousand men and women.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abimelech went to Thebez, and encamped against Thebez, and took it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But there was a strong tower in the midst of the city, and thither fled all the men and women, all the citizens of the city; and they shut it behind them, and went up to the roof of the tower.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abimelech came to the tower, and fought against it, and he drew near to the entrance of the tower to burn it with fire;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and a woman cast the upper stone of a handmill on Abimelech's head, and crushed his skull.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then he called hastily to the young man that carried his armour, and said to him, Draw thy sword, and slay me, that they say not of me, A woman killed him. And his young man thrust him through, and he died.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when the men of Israel saw that Abimelech was dead, they went every man to his place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: AndGod rendered back the wickedness of Abimelech, which he did to his father in slaying his seventy brethren.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the evil of the men of Shechem didGod render back upon their heads; and upon them came the curse of Jotham the son of Jerubbaal.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And after Abimelech, there rose up to save Israel Tola the son of Puah, the son of Dodo, a man of Issachar; and he dwelt in Shamir on mount Ephraim.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he judged Israel twenty-three years; and he died, and was buried in Shamir.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And after him rose up Jair, a Gileadite; and he judged Israel twenty-two years.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he had thirty sons who rode on thirty ass colts; and they had thirty cities, which are called the villages of Jair to this day, which are in the land of Gilead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jair died, and was buried in Kamon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel again did evil in the sight of Jehovah, and served the Baals, and the Ashtoreths, and thegods of Syria, and thegods of Zidon, and thegods of Moab, and thegods of the children of Ammon, and thegods of the Philistines; and they forsook Jehovah, and served him not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the anger of Jehovah was hot against Israel, and he sold him into the hand of the Philistines, and into the hand of the children of Ammon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they oppressed and crushed the children of Israel in that year; eighteen years [they oppressed] all the children of Israel that were beyond the Jordan in the land of the Amorites, which is in Gilead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Ammon passed over the Jordan to fight also against Judah, and against Benjamin, and against the house of Ephraim; and Israel was greatly distressed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel cried to Jehovah, saying, We have sinned against thee, both because we have forsaken ourGod, and also served the Baals.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to the children of Israel, Did I not [save you] from the Egyptians, and from the Amorites, from the children of Ammon, and from the Philistines?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The Zidonians also, and Amalek and Maon oppressed you, and ye cried to me, and I saved you out of their hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But ye have forsaken me, and served othergods; therefore I will save you no more.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Go and cry to thegods that ye have chosen: let them save you in the time of your trouble.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel said to Jehovah, We have sinned. Do thou unto us according to all that is good in thy sight; only deliver us, we pray thee, this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they put away the strangegods from among them, and served Jehovah; and his soul was grieved for the misery of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Ammon were called together and encamped in Gilead; and the children of Israel gathered together and encamped in Mizpeh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the people, the chief men of Gilead, said one to another, Who is the man that will begin to fight against the children of Ammon? he shall be head over all the inhabitants of Gilead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now Jephthah the Gileadite was a mighty man of valour, and he was the son of a harlot; and Gilead had begotten Jephthah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Gilead's wife bore him sons; and when his wife's sons were grown, they expelled Jephthah, and said to him, Thou shalt not inherit in our father's house; for thou art the son of another woman.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Jephthah fled from his brethren, and dwelt in the land of Tob. And vain men were gathered to Jephthah, and they made expeditions with him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass after some time, that the children of Ammon fought with Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when the children of Ammon made war against Israel, the elders of Gilead went to fetch Jephthah out of the land of Tob.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said to Jephthah, Come, and be our captain, that we may fight against the children of Ammon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jephthah said to the elders of Gilead, Did not ye hate me, and expel me out of my father's house? and why are ye come to me now when ye are in trouble?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the elders of Gilead said to Jephthah, Therefore we have returned to thee now, that thou mayest go with us, and fight against the children of Ammon, and be head over all of us the inhabitants of Gilead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jephthah said to the elders of Gilead, If ye take me back to fight against the children of Ammon, and Jehovah give them up before me, shall I be your head?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the elders of Gilead said unto Jephthah, Jehovah be witness between us, if we do not so according to thy words!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Jephthah went with the elders of Gilead, and the people made him head and captain over them; and Jephthah uttered all his words before Jehovah in Mizpah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jephthah sent messengers to the king of the children of Ammon, saying, What hast thou to do with me, that thou art come against me to fight against my land?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king of the children of Ammon said to the messengers of Jephthah, Because Israel took away my land, when they came up out of Egypt, from the Arnon even unto the Jabbok and unto the Jordan; and now restore it peaceably.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jephthah sent messengers again to the king of the children of Ammon,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and said to him, Thus saith Jephthah: Israel took not away the land of Moab, nor the land of the children of Ammon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when they came up from Egypt, then Israel walked through the wilderness as far as the Red sea, and came to Kadesh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Israel sent messengers to the king of Edom, saying, Let me, I pray thee, pass through thy land; but the king of Edom would not hearken. And they also sent to the king of Moab; and he would not. And Israel abode in Kadesh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they walked through the wilderness, and went round the land of Edom and the land of Moab, and came by the east of the land of Moab, and encamped beyond the Arnon, but came not within the border of Moab, for the Arnon is the border of Moab.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Israel sent messengers to Sihon king of the Amorites, the king of Heshbon, and Israel said to him, Let us pass, we pray thee, through thy land unto my place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Sihon trusted not Israel, to let him pass through his border, and Sihon gathered all his people, and they encamped in Jahzah; and he fought with Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah theGod of Israel gave Sihon and all his people into the hand of Israel, and they smote them; and Israel took possession of the whole land of the Amorites, who dwelt in that country.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they possessed all the borders of the Amorites, from the Arnon unto the Jabbok, and from the wilderness unto the Jordan.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now Jehovah theGod of Israel has dispossessed the Amorites from before his people Israel, and shouldest thou take possession of it?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Dost not thou possess what Chemosh thygod puts thee in possession of? and whatever Jehovah ourGod has dispossessed before us, that will we possess.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now art thou indeed better than Balak the son of Zippor, king of Moab? did he ever strive with Israel? did he ever fight against them?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: While Israel dwelt in Heshbon and its dependent villages, and in Aroer and its dependent villages, and in all the cities that are along the banks of the Arnon, three hundred years — why did ye not recover [them] within that time?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So I have not sinned against thee, but it is thou who doest me wrong in making war against me. Jehovah, the Judge, be judge this day between the children of Israel and the children of Ammon!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the king of the children of Ammon hearkened not to the words of Jephthah that he had sent him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then the Spirit of Jehovah came upon Jephthah, and he passed through Gilead and Manasseh, and passed to Mizpeh of Gilead, and from Mizpeh of Gilead he passed over to the children of Ammon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jephthah vowed a vow to Jehovah, and said, If thou wilt without fail give the children of Ammon into my hand,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: then shall that which cometh forth from the door of my house to meet me, when I return in peace from the children of Ammon, be Jehovah's, and I will offer it up for a burnt-offering.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jephthah passed over to the children of Ammon to fight against them; and Jehovah gave them into his hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he smote them from Aroer until thou come to Minnith, twenty cities, even unto Abel-Cheramim, with a very great slaughter; and the children of Ammon were subdued before the children of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jephthah came to Mizpah to his house, and behold, his daughter came out to meet him with tambours and with dances; and she was an only child: besides her he had neither son nor daughter.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass, when he saw her, that he rent his garments, and said, Alas, my daughter! thou hast brought me very low, and thou art one of them that trouble me; for I have opened my mouth to Jehovah, and I cannot go back.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she said to him, My father, if thou hast opened thy mouth to Jehovah, do to me according to that which has proceeded out of thy mouth; forasmuch as Jehovah has taken vengeance for thee upon thine enemies, upon the children of Ammon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she said to her father, Let this thing be done for me: leave me alone two months, that I may go and descend to the mountains, and bewail my virginity, I and my companions.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Go. And he sent her away for two months. And she went with her companions, and bewailed her virginity upon the mountains.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass at the end of two months, that she returned to her father, and he performed on her the vow that he had vowed; and she had known no man. And it became a fixed custom in Israel,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that from year to year the daughters of Israel go to celebrate the daughter of Jephthah the Gileadite four days in the year.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the men of Ephraim were called together, and passed over northwards, and said to Jephthah, Why didst thou pass over to fight against the children of Ammon, and didst not call us to go with thee? We will burn thy house upon thee with fire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jephthah said to them, I was at great strife, I and my people, with the children of Ammon; and I called you, but ye saved me not out of their hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when I saw that ye would not save me, I put my life in my hand, and passed over against the children of Ammon, and Jehovah gave them into my hand. Why then are ye come up to me this day, to fight against me?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jephthah gathered together all the men of Gilead, and fought with Ephraim; and the men of Gilead smote Ephraim, because they said, Ye, Gilead, ye are fugitives of Ephraim in the midst of Ephraim, [and] in the midst of Manasseh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Gilead took the fords of the Jordan before Ephraim; and it came to pass that when the fugitives of Ephraim said, Let me go over, the men of Gilead said to him, Art thou an Ephraimite? and he said, No.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then they said to him, Say now Shibboleth! and he said, Sibboleth, and did not manage to pronounce [it] rightly. Then they took him, and slaughtered him at the fords of the Jordan. And there fell at that time of Ephraim forty-two thousand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jephthah judged Israel six years. And Jephthah the Gileadite died, and was buried in [one of] the cities of Gilead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And after him Ibzan of Bethlehem judged Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he had thirty sons; and thirty daughters he sent out of the house, and thirty daughters he took in from abroad for his sons. And he judged Israel seven years.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Ibzan died, and was buried at Bethlehem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And after him Elon, the Zebulonite, judged Israel; and he judged Israel ten years.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Elon the Zebulonite died, and was buried in Ajalon in the land of Zebulun.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And after him Abdon the son of Hillel, the Pirathonite, judged Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he had forty sons and thirty grandsons, who rode on seventy ass colts. And he judged Israel eight years.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abdon the son of Hillel, the Pirathonite, died, and was buried in Pirathon in the land of Ephraim, in the hill-country of the Amalekites.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel again did evil in the sight of Jehovah; and Jehovah gave them into the hand of the Philistines forty years.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there was a certain man of Zoreah, of the family of the Danites, and his name was Manoah. And his wife was barren and did not bear.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Angel of Jehovah appeared to the woman, and said to her, Behold now, thou art barren and bearest not; but thou shalt conceive and bear a son.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now beware, I pray thee, and drink not wine nor strong drink, and eat nothing unclean.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For lo, thou shalt conceive, and bear a son, and no razor shall come on his head; for the boy shall be a Nazarite ofGod from the womb; and he shall begin to save Israel out of the hand of the Philistines.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the woman came and told her husband, saying, A man ofGod came to me, and his appearance was like the appearance of an angel ofGod, very terrible; but I did not ask him whence he was, neither did he tell me his name.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to me, Behold, thou shalt conceive and bear a son; and now drink not wine nor strong drink, and eat not anything unclean; for the boy shall be a Nazarite ofGod from the womb to the day of his death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Manoah prayed to Jehovah, and said, Ah Lord! let the man ofGod which thou didst send come again unto us, I pray thee, and teach us what we shall do unto the child that shall be born.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: AndGod hearkened to the voice of Manoah; and the Angel ofGod came again to the woman whilst she sat in the field; but Manoah her husband was not with her.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then the woman hasted and ran, and informed her husband, and said to him, Behold, the man has appeared to me, that came to me that day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Manoah rose up and went after his wife, and came to the man, and said to him, Art thou the man that didst speak to the woman? And he said, I am.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Manoah said, When thy words then come to pass, what shall be the child's manner and his doing?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Angel of Jehovah said to Manoah, Of all that I said unto the woman let her beware:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: she shall not eat of anything that cometh of the vine, neither shall she drink wine or strong drink, nor eat anything unclean: all that I commanded her shall she observe.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Manoah said to the Angel of Jehovah, I pray thee, let us detain thee, and we will make ready a kid of the goats for thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Angel of Jehovah said to Manoah, Though thou shouldest detain me, I will not eat of thy bread; and if thou wilt offer a burnt-offering, thou shalt offer it up to Jehovah. For Manoah knew not that he was the Angel of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Manoah said to the Angel of Jehovah, What is thy name, that when thy word cometh to pass we may do thee honour?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Angel of Jehovah said to him, How is it that thou askest after my name, seeing it is wonderful?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Manoah took the kid and the oblation, and offered it up to Jehovah upon the rock. And he did wondrously, and Manoah and his wife looked on.

translations.name: Darby Bible
