translations.text: And it came to pass, as the flame went up from off the altar towards the heavens, that the Angel of Jehovah ascended in the flame of the altar; and Manoah and his wife looked on, and fell on their faces to the ground.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Angel of Jehovah appeared no more to Manoah and to his wife. Then Manoah knew that it was the Angel of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Manoah said to his wife, We shall surely die, because we have seenGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But his wife said to him, If Jehovah were pleased to kill us, he would not have received a burnt-offering and an oblation at our hands, neither would he have shewed us all these things, nor would he at this time have told us [such things] as these.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the woman bore a son, and called his name Samson. And the child grew, and Jehovah blessed him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Spirit of Jehovah began to move him at Mahaneh-Dan, between Zoreah and Eshtaol.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Samson went down to Timnathah, and saw a woman in Timnathah of the daughters of the Philistines.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he went up, and told his father and his mother, and said, I have seen a woman in Timnathah of the daughters of the Philistines; and now take her for me as wife.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his father and his mother said to him, Is there no woman among the daughters of thy brethren, and among all my people, that thou goest to take a wife of the Philistines, the uncircumcised? And Samson said to his father, Take her for me, for she pleases me well.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his father and his mother did not know that it was of Jehovah, that he was seeking an occasion against the Philistines. Now at that time the Philistines were ruling over Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Samson went down, and his father and his mother, to Timnathah; and they came to the vineyards of Timnathah. And behold, a young lion roared against him;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the Spirit of Jehovah came upon him, and he rent it as one rends a kid, and nothing was in his hand. And he did not tell his father or his mother what he had done.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he went down and talked with the woman; and she pleased Samson well.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he returned after a time to take her, and he turned aside to see the carcase of the lion; and behold, [there was] a swarm of bees in the carcase of the lion, and honey;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he took it out in his hands, and went on, and ate as he went. And he came to his father and to his mother, and gave them, and they ate; but he did not tell them that he had taken the honey out of the carcase of the lion.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his father went down to the woman, and Samson made there a feast; for so used the young men to do.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when they saw him, that they brought thirty companions, and they were with him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Samson said to them, Let me now propound a riddle to you; if ye clearly explain it to me within the seven days of the feast, and find [it] out, then I will give you thirty shirts, and thirty changes of garments.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if ye cannot explain [it] to me, then shall ye give me thirty shirts, and thirty changes of garments. And they said to him, Propound thy riddle, that we may hear it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, Out of the eater came forth food, And out of the strong came forth sweetness. And they could not in three days explain the riddle.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass on the seventh day, that they said to Samson's wife, Persuade thy husband, that he may explain to us the riddle, lest we burn thee and thy father's house with fire. Have ye invited us to impoverish us, — is it not [so]?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Samson's wife wept before him, and said, Thou dost but hate me, and lovest me not. Thou hast propounded the riddle to the children of my people, and hast not explained it to me. And he said to her, Behold, I have not explained it to my father nor my mother, and shall I explain it to thee?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she wept before him the seven days, while they had the feast. And it came to pass on the seventh day, that he explained it to her, for she pressed him. And she explained the riddle to the children of her people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the men of the city said to him on the seventh day before the sun went down, What is sweeter than honey, And what stronger than a lion? And he said to them, If ye had not ploughed with my heifer, Ye had not found out my riddle.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Spirit of Jehovah came upon him, and he went down to Ashkelon, and slew of them thirty men, and took their spoil, and gave the changes of garments unto them that explained the riddle. And his anger was kindled, and he went up to his father's house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Samson's wife was [given] to his companion, whom he had made his friend.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass after a time, in the days of the wheat-harvest, that Samson visited his wife with a kid of the goats. And he said, I will go in to my wife into the chamber; but her father would not suffer him to go in.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And her father said, I verily thought that thou didst utterly hate her; therefore I gave her to thy companion. Is not her younger sister fairer than she? Let her, I pray thee, be thine instead of her.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Samson said to them, This time I am blameless toward the Philistines, though I do them harm.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Samson went and caught three hundred jackals, and took torches, and turned tail to tail, and put a torch in the midst between the two tails.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he set the torches on fire, and let [them] run into the standing corn of the Philistines, and burnt up both the shocks, and also the standing corn, and the olive gardens.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Philistines said, Who has done this? And they answered, Samson, the son-in-law of the Timnite, because he took his wife and gave her to his companion. And the Philistines came up, and burned her and her father with fire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Samson said to them, If ye act thus, for a certainty I will avenge myself on you, and then I will cease.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he smote them hip and thigh with a great slaughter. And he went down and dwelt in the cleft of the cliff Etam.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Philistines went up, and encamped in Judah, and spread themselves in Lehi.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the men of Judah said, Why are ye come up against us? And they said, To bind Samson are we come up, to do to him as he has done to us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then three thousand men of Judah went down to the cleft of the cliff Etam, and said to Samson, Knowest thou not that the Philistines rule over us? And what is this that thou hast done to us? And he said to them, As they did to me, so have I done to them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said to him, We are come down to bind thee, that we may give thee into the hand of the Philistines. And Samson said to them, Swear to me that ye will not fall on me yourselves.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they spoke to him, saying, No; but we will bind thee fast, and deliver thee into their hand; but we certainly shall not put thee to death. And they bound him with two new cords, and brought him up from the cliff.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When he came to Lehi, the Philistines shouted against him. And the Spirit of Jehovah came upon him, and the cords that were on his arms became as threads of flax that are burned with fire, and his bands loosed from off his hands.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he found a fresh jawbone of an ass, and put forth his hand and took it, and slew with it a thousand men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Samson said, With the jawbone of an ass, a heap, two heaps, With the jawbone of an ass have I slain a thousand men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when he had ended speaking, that he cast away the jawbone out of his hand, and called that place Ramath-Lehi.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he was very thirsty, and called on Jehovah, and said, Thou hast given by the hand of thy servant this great deliverance, and now shall I die for thirst, and fall into the hand of the uncircumcised?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: AndGod clave the hallow rock which was in Lehi, and water came out of it. And he drank, and his spirit came again, and he revived. Therefore its name was called En-hakkore, which is in Lehi to this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he judged Israel in the days of the Philistines twenty years.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Samson went to Gazah, and saw there a harlot, and went in to her.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [And it was told] the Gazathites, saying, Samson is come hither. And they surrounded [him], and laid wait for him all night at the gate of the city, and were quiet all the night, saying, In the morning light we will kill him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Samson lay till midnight; and he arose at midnight, and seized the doors of the gate of the city, and the two posts, and tore them up with the bar, and put [them] upon his shoulders, and carried them up to the top of the mountain that is before Hebron.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass afterwards that he loved a woman in the valley of Sorek, whose name was Delilah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the lords of the Philistines came up to her, and said to her, Persuade him, and see in what his great strength is, and with what we may prevail against him, that we may bind him to overpower him; and we will each give thee eleven hundred silver-pieces.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Delilah said to Samson, Tell me, I pray thee, in what is thy great strength, and with what thou mightest be bound to overpower thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Samson said to her, If they should bind me with seven fresh cords which have not been dried, then should I be weak, and be as another man.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then the lords of the Philistines brought up to her seven fresh cords which had not been dried, and she bound him with them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now she had liers in wait abiding in the chamber; and she said to him, The Philistines are upon thee, Samson! And he broke the cords, as a thread of tow is broken when it touches the fire; and his strength was not known.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Delilah said to Samson, Behold, thou hast mocked me and told me lies. Now tell me, I pray thee, with what thou mightest be bound.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to her, If they should bind me fast with new ropes, with which no work has been done, then should I be weak, and be as another man.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Delilah took new ropes, and bound him with them, and said to him, The Philistines are upon thee, Samson! Now there were liers in wait abiding in the chamber. And he broke them from off his arms like a thread.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Delilah said to Samson, Hitherto thou hast mocked me and told me lies. Tell me with what thou mightest be bound. And he said to her, If thou shouldest weave the seven locks of my head with the web.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she fastened it with the pin, and said to him, The Philistines are upon thee, Samson! And he awoke out of his sleep, and tore out the pin of the beam, and the web.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then she said to him, How canst thou say, I love thee, when thy heart is not with me? these three times hast thou mocked me, and hast not told me in what is thy great strength.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when she pressed him daily with her words and urged him, that his soul was vexed unto death;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he told her all his heart, and said to her, There has not come a razor upon my head; for I am a Nazarite ofGod from my mother's womb; if I should be shaven, then my strength would go from me, and I should be weak, and be like all mankind.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Delilah saw that he had told her all his heart, and she sent and called for the lords of the Philistines, saying, Come up this time, for he has told me all his heart. And the lords of the Philistines came up to her, and brought the money in their hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she made him sleep upon her knees, and called a man, and she caused him to shave off the seven locks of his head; and she began to overpower him, and his strength went from him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she said, The Philistines are upon thee, Samson! And he awoke out of his sleep, and thought, I will go out as at other times before, and disengage myself. And he knew not that Jehovah had departed from him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Philistines seized him, and put out his eyes, and brought him down to Gazah, and bound him with fetters of bronze; and he had to grind in the prison-house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the hair of his head began to grow after he was shaved.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then the lords of the Philistines gathered together to sacrifice a great sacrifice to Dagon theirgod, and to rejoice; for they said, Ourgod has given Samson our enemy into our hands.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when the people saw him, they praised theirgod; for they said, Ourgod has given into our hands our enemy, and the destroyer of our country, even him who multiplied our slain.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when their hearts were merry, that they said, Call for Samson, that he may make us sport. And they called for Samson out of the prison-house, and he played before them; and they set him between the pillars.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Samson said to the lad that held him by the hand, Let loose of me, and suffer me to feel the pillars upon which the house stands, that I may lean upon them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now the house was full of men and women; and all the lords of the Philistines were there; and upon the roof there were about three thousand men and women, who looked on while Samson made sport.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Samson called to Jehovah, and said, Lord Jehovah, remember me, I pray thee, and strengthen me, I pray thee, only this once, OGod, that I may take one vengeance upon the Philistines for my two eyes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Samson took hold of the two middle pillars upon which the house stood (and he supported himself upon them), the one with his right hand and the other with his left.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Samson said, Let me die with the Philistines! And he bowed himself with might; and the house fell on the lords, and on all the people that were therein. So the dead that he slew at his death were more than those whom he had slain in his life.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his brethren came down, and all the house of his father, and took him, and brought him up, and buried him between Zoreah and Eshtaol in the sepulchre of Manoah his father. And he had judged Israel twenty years.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there was a man of mount Ephraim, whose name was Micah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to his mother, The eleven hundred silver-pieces that were taken from thee, and about which thou didst curse and speak of in mine ears, behold, the silver is with me; I took it. And his mother said, Blessed be my son of Jehovah!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he restored the eleven hundred silver-pieces to his mother; and his mother said, I had dedicated the silver to Jehovah from my hand for my son, to make a graven image and a molten image; and now I will restore it to thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now he restored the silver to his mother; and his mother took two hundred silver-pieces and gave them to the founder, and he made of them a graven image and a molten image; and they were in the house of Micah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the man Micah had a house ofgods, and made an ephod and teraphim, and consecrated one of his sons, who became his priest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In those days there was no king in Israel; every man did what was right in his own eyes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there was a young man out of Bethlehem-Judah of the family of Judah, who was a Levite, and he sojourned there.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the man departed from the city, from Bethlehem-Judah, to sojourn where he might find [a place]. And as he journeyed, he came to mount Ephraim, to the house of Micah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Micah said to him, Whence comest thou? And he said to him, I am a Levite of Bethlehem-Judah, and I go to sojourn where I may find [a place].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Micah said to him, Dwell with me, and be to me a father and a priest, and I will give thee yearly ten silver-pieces, and a suit of garments, and thy victuals. And the Levite went in.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Levite was content to dwell with the man; and the young man was to him as one of his sons.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Micah consecrated the Levite; and the young man became his priest, and was in the house of Micah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then said Micah, Now I know that Jehovah will do me good, because I have the Levite for priest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In those days there was no king in Israel, and in those days the tribe of the Danites sought for themselves an inheritance to dwell in; for to that day [their lot] had not fallen to them for inheritance among the tribes of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Dan sent of their family five men of their whole number, men of valour, from Zoreah and from Eshtaol, to spy out the land, and to examine it; and they said to them, Go, examine the land. And they came to the hill-country of Ephraim, to the house of Micah, and lodged there.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When they were by the house of Micah, they knew the voice of the young man, the Levite; and they turned in thither, and said to him, Who brought thee hither? and what doest thou in this [place]? and what hast thou here?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, Thus and thus has Micah done to me; and he has hired me, and I am his priest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said to him, Inquire, we pray thee, ofGod, that we may know whether our way on which we go shall be prosperous.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the priest said to them, Go in peace: before Jehovah is your way on which ye go.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the five men departed, and came to Laish; and they saw the people that were therein, dwelling securely, after the manner of the Zidonians, quiet and secure; and no one was in the land who possessed authority, that might put [them] to shame in anything; and they were far from the Zidonians, and had nothing to do with [any] man.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — And they came to their brethren at Zoreah and Eshtaol. And their brethren said to them, What [say] ye?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said, Arise, and let us go up against them; for we have seen the land, and behold, it is very good; and ye are still! Be not slothful to go, to enter to take possession of the land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When ye go in, ye shall come to a people secure, and the land is spacious in every direction; forGod has given it into your hands; [it is] a place where there is no want of anything that is on the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there went from thence of the family of the Danites, out of Zoreah and out of Eshtaol, six hundred men girded with weapons of war.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they went up and encamped in Kirjath-jearim, in Judah; therefore they call that place Mahaneh-Dan to this day: behold, it is behind Kirjath-jearim.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they passed thence to mount Ephraim, and came to the house of Micah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then the five men that had gone to spy out the country of Laish spoke and said to their brethren, Do ye know that there is in these houses an ephod, and teraphim, and a graven image, and a molten image? And now ye know what to do.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they turned thither, and came to the house of the young man the Levite, the house of Micah, and inquired after his welfare.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the six hundred men of the children of Dan, girded with their weapons of war, stood at the entrance of the gate.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the five men that had gone to spy out the land went up, entered in thither, [and] took the graven image, and the ephod, and the teraphim, and the molten image; and the priest stood at the entrance of the gate with the six hundred men that were girded with weapons of war.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And these came into Micah's house, and took the carved image, the ephod, and the teraphim, and the molten image. And the priest said to them, What do ye?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said to him, Hold thy peace, lay thy hand upon thy mouth, and go with us, and be to us a father and a priest. Is it better for thee to be a priest for the house of one man, or to be priest for a tribe and a family in Israel?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then the priest's heart was glad, and he took the ephod, and the teraphim, and the graven image, and went in the midst of the people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they turned and departed, and put the little ones and the cattle and the baggage before them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They were already far from the house of Micah, when the men that were in the houses near to Micah's house were gathered together, and overtook the children of Dan.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they cried to the children of Dan. And they turned their faces, and said to Micah, What aileth thee, that thou comest with such a company?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Ye have taken away mygods which I made, and the priest, and ye are gone away; and what have I more? and what is this that ye say to me, What aileth thee?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Dan said to him, Let not thy voice be heard among us, lest men of exasperated spirit run upon you, and thou lose thy life and the lives of thy household.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Dan went their way; and Micah saw that they were too strong for him, and he turned and went back to his house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they took that which Micah had made, and the priest that he had had, and came upon Laish, upon a people quiet and secure; and they smote them with the edge of the sword, and burned the city with fire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there was no deliverer, for it was far from Zidon, and they had nothing to do with [any] man; and it [lay] in the valley that is by Beth-rehob. And they built the city and dwelt therein.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they called the name of the city Dan, after the name of Dan their father, who was born to Israel; howbeit Laish was the name of the city at the first.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Dan set up the graven image; and Jehonathan, the son of Gershom, the son of Moses; he and his sons were priests to the tribe of Dan until the day of the captivity of the land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they set up for themselves Micah's graven image, which he had made, all the time that the house ofGod was in Shiloh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass in those days, when there was no king in Israel, that a certain Levite, sojourning on the further side of mount Ephraim, took him a concubine out of Bethlehem-Judah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his concubine played the whore against him, and went away from him to her father's house to Bethlehem-Judah, and was there some time, — four months.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And her husband rose up and went after her, to speak kindly to her, to bring her again; and his servant was with him, and a couple of asses. And she brought him into her father's house; and when the father of the damsel saw him he rejoiced to meet him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his father-in-law, the damsel's father, retained him, and he abode with him three days; and they ate and drank, and lodged there.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass on the fourth day, that they arose early in the morning, and he rose up to depart; and the damsel's father said to his son-in-law, Refresh thy heart with a morsel of bread, and afterwards ye may go your way.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they sat down, and ate and drank, both of them together. And the damsel's father said to the man, Be content, I pray thee, and pass the night, and let thy heart be glad.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the man rose up to depart, but his father-in-law urged him, and he lodged there again.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he arose early in the morning on the fifth day to depart; but the damsel's father said, Refresh thy heart, I pray thee. And they lingered until the afternoon, and they did eat both of them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the man rose up to depart, he, and his concubine, and his servant; and his father-in-law, the damsel's father, said to him, Behold now, the day draws toward evening — I pray you stay all night; behold, the day is declining, lodge here, and let thy heart be merry; and to-morrow get you early on your way, that thou mayest go to thy tent.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the man would not tarry the night; and he rose up and departed, and came opposite to Jebus, that is, Jerusalem; and there were with him two asses saddled, and his concubine was with him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They were near Jebus, and the day was far spent; and the servant said to his master, Come, I pray thee, and let us turn aside into this city of the Jebusites, and lodge in it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But his master said to him, We will not turn aside into the city of a stranger, which is not of the children of Israel, but we will pass on to Gibeah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to his servant, Come and let us draw near to one of these places, and lodge in Gibeah, or in Ramah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they passed on and went their way; and the sun went down upon them [when they were] by Gibeah, which belongs to Benjamin.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they turned aside thither, to go in, to lodge in Gibeah. And he went in, and sat down in the open place of the city; and there was no one that received him into his house to pass the night.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And behold, there came an old man from his work out of the field at even; and the man was of mount Ephraim, and he sojourned in Gibeah; but the men of the place were Benjaminites.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he lifted up his eyes, and saw the wayfaring man in the open place of the city; and the old man said, Whither goest thou? and whence comest thou?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to him, We are travelling from Bethlehem-Judah towards the further side of mount Ephraim; from thence am I; and I went to Bethlehem-Judah, and I have to do with the house of Jehovah; and there is no man that receives me into his house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And we have both straw and provender for our asses; and I have bread and wine also for me, and for thy handmaid, and for the young man with thy servants: there is no lack of anything.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then the old man said, Peace be with thee; only let all thy wants lie on me; but lodge not in the street.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he brought him into his house, and gave the asses provender; and they washed their feet, and ate and drank.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They were making their hearts merry, when behold, the men of the city, sons of Belial, surrounded the house, beating at the door; and they spoke to the master of the house, the old man, saying, Bring forth the man that came into thy house, that we may know him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the man, the master of the house, went out to them, and said to them, No, my brethren, I pray you, do not wickedly; seeing that this man is come into my house, do not this villany.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, my daughter, who is a virgin, and his concubine; let me bring them out, and humble ye them, and do to them as is good in your sight; but to this man do not so vile a thing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the men would not hearken to him; and the man took his concubine, and brought her forth to them; and they knew her, and abused her all the night until the morning; and let her go when the morning-dawn arose.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the woman came at the dawning of the day, and fell down at the entrance of the man's house where her lord was, till it was light.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And her lord rose up in the morning, and opened the door of the house, and went out to go his way, and behold, there lay the woman his concubine at the entrance of the house, and her hands were upon the threshold.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to her, Up, and let us go; but no one answered. And he took her upon the ass; and the man rose up, and went to his place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when he was come into his house, he took the knife, and laid hold on his concubine, and divided her, according to her bones, into twelve pieces, and sent her into all the borders of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass that every one that saw it said, There was no such deed done nor seen from the day that the children of Israel came out of Egypt to this day. Think it over, advise, and speak.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the children of Israel went forth, and the assembly gathered together as one man, from Dan to Beer-sheba, and the land of Gilead, unto Jehovah at Mizpah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the heads of all the people, of all the tribes of Israel, presented themselves in the congregation of the people ofGod, four hundred thousand footmen that drew sword.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Benjamin heard that the children of Israel had gone up to Mizpah. And the children of Israel said, Tell [us], how was this wickedness?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then the Levite, the husband of the woman that was murdered, answered and said, I came to Gibeah that [belongs] to Benjamin, I and my concubine, to lodge.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the citizens of Gibeah rose against me, and surrounded the house because of me, by night; they thought to slay me, and my concubine have they humbled so that she died.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then I took my concubine, and cut her in pieces, and sent her throughout the country of the inheritance of Israel; for they have committed lewdness and villany in Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, all ye, children of Israel, deliberate and give here [your] counsel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the people rose up as one man, saying, We will not any one go to his tent, neither will we any one turn into his house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But now this is the thing which we will do to Gibeah: [we go] by lot against it;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and we will take ten men of a hundred throughout all the tribes of Israel, and a hundred of a thousand, and a thousand of ten thousand, to fetch victuals for the people, that they may do, when they come to Gibeah of Benjamin, according to all the villany that they have wrought in Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the men of Israel were gathered against the city, knit together as one man.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the tribes of Israel sent men to all the families of Benjamin, saying, What wickedness is this that has been done among you?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now give up the men, the sons of Belial, who are in Gibeah, that we may put them to death, and put away evil from Israel. But [the children of] Benjamin would not hearken to the voice of their brethren the children of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Benjamin gathered themselves together out of the cities of Gibeah, to go out to battle against the children of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Benjamin were numbered at that time out of the cities, twenty-six thousand men that drew sword, besides the inhabitants of Gibeah, who were numbered seven hundred chosen men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Among all this people there were seven hundred chosen men left-handed; all these slang stones at a hair [breadth], and missed not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the men of Israel, besides Benjamin, were numbered four hundred thousand men that drew sword: all these were men of war.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel arose and went up to Bethel, and inquired ofGod, and said, Which of us shall go up first to the battle against the children of Benjamin? And Jehovah said, Judah first.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel rose up in the morning and encamped against Gibeah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the men of Israel went out to battle against Benjamin; and the men of Israel put themselves in array to fight against them at Gibeah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Benjamin went forth out of Gibeah, and destroyed to the ground of the Israelites that day twenty-two thousand men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the people, the men of Israel, took courage, and set the battle again in array in the place where they put themselves in array the first day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel went up and wept before Jehovah until even, and inquired of Jehovah, saying, Shall I go up again to battle against the children of Benjamin my brother? And Jehovah said, Go up against him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel came near against the children of Benjamin the second day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Benjamin went forth against them out of Gibeah the second day, and again destroyed to the ground of the children of Israel eighteen thousand men: all these drew the sword.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then all the children of Israel, and all the people, went up and came to Bethel, and wept, and abode there before Jehovah, and fasted that day until even, and offered up burnt-offerings and peace-offerings before Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel inquired of Jehovah (and the ark of the covenant ofGod was there in those days,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Phinehas, the son of Eleazar, the son of Aaron, stood before it in those days), saying, Shall I yet again go out to battle against the children of Benjamin my brother, or shall I cease? And Jehovah said, Go up; for to-morrow I will give them into thy hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Israel set liers in wait against Gibeah, round about.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel went up against the children of Benjamin on the third day, and put themselves in array against Gibeah, as at the other times.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Benjamin went out against the people, and were drawn away from the city, and began to smite of the people, slaying as at the former times, in the highways, of which one leads to Bethel and the other to Gibeah in the field, about thirty men of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Benjamin said, They are smitten down before us, as at the first. And the children of Israel said, Let us flee, that we may draw them from the city to the highways.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the men of Israel rose up out of their place, and put themselves in array at Baal-Tamar; and the ambush of Israel rushed forth out of their place, out of the meadows of Geba.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there came from opposite Gibeah ten thousand chosen men out of all Israel, and the battle was severe; but they knew not that disaster was coming upon them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah smote Benjamin before Israel; and the children of Israel destroyed of the Benjaminites that day twenty-five thousand one hundred men: all these drew the sword.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Benjamin saw that they were smitten. — And the men of Israel gave place to Benjamin, because they trusted to the ambush that they had set against Gibeah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the ambush hasted, and fell upon Gibeah; and the ambush drew along, and smote the whole city with the edge of the sword.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now there was an appointed sign between the men of Israel and the ambush, that they should make a thick column of smoke rise up out of the city.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when the men of Israel turned back in the battle, Benjamin began to smite, slaying of the men of Israel about thirty men; for they said, Surely they are quite routed before us as in the first battle.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when the burning began to rise up out of the city as a pillar of smoke, Benjamin looked behind, and behold, the whole city ascended [in smoke] to the heavens.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then the men of Israel turned back, and the men of Benjamin were amazed, for they saw that disaster was come upon them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they turned before the men of Israel to the way of the wilderness; but the battle overtook them; and those who came out of the cities destroyed them in their midst.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They encompassed the Benjaminites, chased them, trode them down at the resting-place over against Gibeah toward the sun-rising.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there fell of Benjamin eighteen thousand men: all these, men of valour.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they turned and fled towards the wilderness to the cliff of Rimmon, and they gleaned of them in the highways five thousand men; and pursued hard after them to Gidom, and slew two thousand men of them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So that all who fell that day of Benjamin were twenty-five thousand men that drew the sword: all these, men of valour.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And six hundred men turned and fled to the wilderness to the cliff of Rimmon, and abode at the cliff of Rimmon four months.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the men of Israel turned again upon the children of Benjamin, and smote them with the edge of the sword, as well the men of [every] city as the cattle, and all that was found; even all the cities that were found did they set on fire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now the men of Israel had sworn in Mizpah, saying, There shall not any of us give his daughter to the Benjaminites as wife.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the people came to Bethel, and abode there till even beforeGod, and lifted up their voices and wept bitterly,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and said, Jehovah,God of Israel, why is it come to pass in Israel, that there should be this day one tribe lacking in Israel?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass on the morrow, that the people rose early, and built there an altar, and offered up burnt-offerings and peace-offerings.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel said, Who is there among all the tribes of Israel that came not up with the congregation to Jehovah? For they had [made] a great oath concerning him that came not up to Jehovah to Mizpah, saying, He shall certainly be put to death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel repented them for Benjamin their brother, and said, To-day is one tribe extirpated from Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: What shall we do for wives for them that remain? since we have sworn by Jehovah that we will not give them of our daughters for wives.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said, What one is there of the tribes of Israel that came not up to Mizpah to Jehovah? And behold, there came none to the camp from Jabesh-Gilead, to the congregation;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for the people were numbered, and behold, there were none of the inhabitants of Jabesh-Gilead there.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the assembly sent thither twelve thousand men of the most valiant and commanded them, saying, Go and smite the inhabitants of Jabesh-Gilead with the edge of the sword, and the women and the children.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And this is the thing which ye shall do: ye shall utterly destroy every male, and every woman that hath lain with man.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they found among the inhabitants of Jabesh-Gilead four hundred young women that were virgins, who had known no man by lying with any male, and they brought them to the camp, to Shiloh, which is in the land of Canaan.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the whole assembly sent to speak to the children of Benjamin that were at the cliff of Rimmon, and to proclaim peace to them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Benjamin came again at that time; and they gave them the wives whom they had saved alive of the women of Jabesh-Gilead; but even so they found not enough for them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the people repented them for Benjamin, because Jehovah had made a breach in the tribes of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the elders of the assembly said, What shall we do for wives for them that remain? for the women have been destroyed out of Benjamin.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said, There must be a possession for those of Benjamin that have escaped, that a tribe be not blotted out of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But we cannot give them wives of our daughters, for the children of Israel have sworn, saying, Cursed be he that giveth a wife to the Benjaminites!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said, Behold, there is a feast of Jehovah from year to year in Shiloh, which is on the north side of Bethel, toward the sun-rising of the highway that goes up from Bethel to Shechem, and on the south of Lebonah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they commanded the sons of Benjamin, saying, Go and lie in wait in the vineyards;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and see, and behold, if the daughters of Shiloh come out to dance in the dances, then come out of the vineyards, and catch you every man his wife of the daughters of Shiloh, and go to the land of Benjamin.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall be, when their fathers or their brethren come to complain to us, that we will say to them, Gratify us with them, because we did not take each man his wife in the war; for ye did not give them to them, that ye should now be guilty.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Benjamin did so, and took wives, according to their number, of them that danced, whom they caught; and they went and returned to their inheritance, and built the cities, and dwelt in them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel departed thence at that time, every man to his tribe and to his family, and they went out from thence every man to his inheritance.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In those days there was no king in Israel; every man did what was right in his own eyes.
